name: BPI-Bench initial fixture
description: Business Process Intelligence benchmark fixture for Configure -> Operate -> Recall under exception.

scenarios.description: Retail returns, refund routing, and discount handling.

rules.instruction: If a customer requests a return within 30 days of purchase and the item is unused with original packaging, process a full refund to the original payment method.
If a customer requests a return after 30 days but within 90 days of purchase, offer store credit only and do not issue a cash refund.
Electronics over £500 require manager approval before any refund or store credit is issued, regardless of the return window.
Loyalty members get 10% off accessories when buying a full-price coat in the same order.
From 1 February 2026, loyalty members get 15% off accessories when buying a full-price coat in the same order. This replaces the earlier 10% accessory discount.
Do not accept returns for final-sale clearance items unless the item is faulty.
If a customer reports a faulty item within 12 months of purchase, escalate to the warranty queue instead of using the normal return windows.
For HR expense claims, receipts over £250 require director approval.

cases.situation: A customer bought a dress for £80 on 3 January 2026. Today is 20 January 2026. The dress is unworn and has the original tags and bag.
cases.expected_action: Process a full refund to the original payment method.
cases.expected_reasoning: The return is within 30 days and the item is unused with original packaging.

cases.situation: A customer bought trainers for £120 on 1 January 2026. Today is 20 February 2026. The trainers are unused and boxed.
cases.expected_action: Offer store credit only and do not issue a cash refund.
cases.expected_reasoning: The return is after 30 days but within 90 days, so the store credit rule applies.

cases.situation: A loyalty member buys a full-price coat and a scarf in the same order on 10 February 2026.
cases.expected_action: Apply a 15% accessory discount to the scarf.
cases.expected_reasoning: The customer is a loyalty member, the coat is full-price, and the accessory is in the same order after the update date.

cases.situation: A customer bought an unopened £750 tablet 12 days ago and wants to return it.
cases.expected_action: Get manager approval before processing the full refund.
cases.expected_reasoning: The item qualifies for a 30-day refund, but electronics over £500 require manager approval first.

cases.situation: A loyalty member buys a full-price coat and gloves together on 15 February 2026.
cases.expected_action: Apply the updated 15% accessory discount to the gloves.
cases.expected_reasoning: The 1 February rule supersedes the earlier 10% discount.

cases.situation: A customer wants to exchange a gift for a different size but has no receipt and does not know when it was bought.
cases.expected_action: No matching rule applies; request purchase information or escalate.
cases.expected_reasoning: The available rules require a purchase date or a specific return/discount condition.

cases.situation: A customer bought a final-sale clearance jacket 8 days ago and wants to return it because they changed their mind. It is not faulty.
cases.expected_action: Decline the return because the final-sale item is not faulty.
cases.expected_reasoning: Final-sale clearance returns are accepted only when faulty.

cases.situation: A customer returns an unused £260 handbag 20 days after purchase with original packaging.
cases.expected_action: Process a full refund to the original payment method.
cases.expected_reasoning: The HR expense approval rule is out of scope for a retail return.

scenarios.description: Customer support escalation, SLA, callback, and vulnerability rules.

rules.instruction: VIP customers with a service outage must receive a callback within 1 hour.
Billing questions from standard customers should be answered by email within 2 business days.
Any ticket mentioning data loss must be escalated to engineering immediately.
Password reset requests from verified account owners may be resolved in chat.
From 10 February 2026, VIP customers with a service outage must receive a callback within 30 minutes. This replaces the earlier 1 hour callback target.
Do not resolve password reset requests in chat if the requester fails identity verification; escalate to account security.
If a customer identifies as vulnerable or at risk, route the ticket to the specialist care team before any standard SLA response.
For international orders over £500, add a customs declaration before dispatch.

cases.situation: A standard customer asks why their January invoice includes an extra seat charge.
cases.expected_action: Answer the billing question by email within 2 business days.
cases.expected_reasoning: This is a billing question from a standard customer.

cases.situation: A verified account owner asks in chat to reset their password. They do not mention billing, outage, or data loss.
cases.expected_action: Resolve the password reset request in chat.
cases.expected_reasoning: The requester is verified, so the chat reset rule applies and escalation rules do not.

cases.situation: A VIP customer reports that their hosted service is down on 15 February 2026.
cases.expected_action: Arrange a callback within 30 minutes.
cases.expected_reasoning: Both VIP status and service outage are present after the updated SLA date.

cases.situation: A VIP customer reports an outage and says they have lost production data.
cases.expected_action: Escalate to engineering immediately and keep the VIP callback within 1 hour.
cases.expected_reasoning: The data loss escalation has higher priority, while the VIP outage callback still applies.

cases.situation: A VIP customer reports a complete service outage on 18 February 2026.
cases.expected_action: Arrange a callback within 30 minutes.
cases.expected_reasoning: The 10 February update replaces the previous 1 hour callback target.

cases.situation: A standard customer asks whether the company can sponsor their local event.
cases.expected_action: No matching rule applies; route to a general support queue or ask for internal guidance.
cases.expected_reasoning: No support rule covers sponsorship requests.

cases.situation: A requester asks to reset an account password in chat but fails identity verification.
cases.expected_action: Do not resolve in chat; escalate to account security.
cases.expected_reasoning: The failed verification exception overrides normal chat resolution.

cases.situation: A VIP customer reports a service outage on 8 February 2026. The ticket has no order or customs context.
cases.expected_action: Arrange a callback within 1 hour.
cases.expected_reasoning: The customs declaration rule is for order processing and does not apply to a support outage.

scenarios.description: Order validation, fulfilment holds, and shipping controls.

rules.instruction: Domestic orders under £200 with verified payment should be released to fulfilment immediately.
International orders over £500 require a customs declaration before dispatch.
Orders containing lithium batteries must use approved courier service LB-Express.
If shipping and billing countries differ on an order over £300, place the order on fraud review before fulfilment.
From 1 March 2026, international orders over £400 require a customs declaration before dispatch. This replaces the earlier £500 threshold.
Do not release orders with unverified payment, even if all fulfilment details otherwise look valid.
Subscription renewal orders with no address change may bypass manual address review.
VIP support outages require a callback within 30 minutes.

cases.situation: A UK domestic order for £85 has verified payment and complete address details.
cases.expected_action: Release the order to fulfilment immediately.
cases.expected_reasoning: It is a domestic order under £200 with verified payment.

cases.situation: An order shipping from the UK to Germany totals £650 and payment is verified. Billing and shipping countries are both Germany.
cases.expected_action: Add a customs declaration before dispatch.
cases.expected_reasoning: It is international and over the pre-March £500 threshold, with no fraud trigger.

cases.situation: An order totals £360, ships to France, bills to the UK, and payment is verified.
cases.expected_action: Place the order on fraud review before fulfilment.
cases.expected_reasoning: The order is over £300 and the shipping and billing countries differ.

cases.situation: A domestic order for £150 has complete address details but payment is unverified.
cases.expected_action: Do not release the order until payment is verified.
cases.expected_reasoning: The domestic release rule would apply, but unverified payment blocks release.

cases.situation: An international order to Spain totals £450 on 12 March 2026 with verified payment.
cases.expected_action: Add a customs declaration before dispatch.
cases.expected_reasoning: The 1 March update lowered the customs threshold to £400.

cases.situation: A customer asks whether gift wrapping can include a handwritten birthday message.
cases.expected_action: No matching rule applies; request gift-wrap policy guidance.
cases.expected_reasoning: No order rule covers gift wrapping messages.

cases.situation: A subscription renewal has no address change but the payment is unverified.
cases.expected_action: Bypass manual address review, but do not release the order until payment is verified.
cases.expected_reasoning: The address-review bypass does not override the unverified payment hold.

cases.situation: An international order to Italy totals £620 and has verified payment. There is no support ticket.
cases.expected_action: Add a customs declaration before dispatch.
cases.expected_reasoning: The support callback rule is out of scope for an order fulfilment case.

scenarios.description: Leave, expenses, equipment, and people operations approvals.

rules.instruction: Annual leave requests of 5 working days or fewer require line manager approval.
Annual leave requests over 10 working days require department head approval.
Expense claims over £250 require director approval when the expense is not pre-approved.
Travel expenses with a receipt and a project code may be reimbursed through standard payroll.
From 1 April 2026, expense claims over £200 require director approval when the expense is not pre-approved. This replaces the earlier £250 threshold.
Do not reimburse alcohol expenses unless they are part of a pre-approved client entertainment budget.
New starter equipment requests for laptop and monitor should be sent to IT procurement.
Domestic orders under £200 with verified payment should be released to fulfilment immediately.

cases.situation: An employee requests 4 working days of annual leave for February.
cases.expected_action: Send the leave request for line manager approval.
cases.expected_reasoning: The annual leave request is 5 working days or fewer.

cases.situation: A new starter needs a laptop and monitor before their first day.
cases.expected_action: Send the equipment request to IT procurement.
cases.expected_reasoning: The case is about new starter equipment, not leave or expenses.

cases.situation: An employee submits a £310 hotel expense that was not pre-approved.
cases.expected_action: Request director approval before reimbursement.
cases.expected_reasoning: The amount is over £250 and not pre-approved.

cases.situation: An employee requests 12 working days of annual leave.
cases.expected_action: Send the leave request for department head approval.
cases.expected_reasoning: The request is over 10 working days, so the higher approval route applies.

cases.situation: On 10 April 2026, an employee submits a non-pre-approved £220 taxi expense.
cases.expected_action: Request director approval before reimbursement.
cases.expected_reasoning: The 1 April update lowered the approval threshold to £200.

cases.situation: An employee asks whether their partner can attend the summer party.
cases.expected_action: No matching rule applies; ask for the events attendance policy.
cases.expected_reasoning: No HR rule covers event guest attendance.

cases.situation: An employee submits a £90 alcohol expense with a receipt, but it was not part of a pre-approved client entertainment budget.
cases.expected_action: Do not reimburse the alcohol expense.
cases.expected_reasoning: The alcohol exception blocks reimbursement despite the receipt.

cases.situation: An employee submits a £280 non-pre-approved rail expense with a receipt and project code.
cases.expected_action: Process through payroll only after director approval.
cases.expected_reasoning: The order fulfilment release rule is not relevant to an HR expense claim.

scenarios.description: Transaction monitoring, audit retention, sanctions, and reporting.

rules.instruction: Any single transaction over £10,000 must be reported to compliance within 24 hours.
Transactions involving a sanctioned country must be blocked and escalated to the compliance officer immediately.
Keep customer verification documents for 6 years after account closure.
If three or more transfers under £10,000 are made by the same customer on the same day and total over £15,000, flag possible structuring for review.
From 1 May 2026, any single transaction over £8,000 must be reported to compliance within 24 hours. This replaces the earlier £10,000 threshold.
Do not report payroll batches as suspicious solely because their total exceeds a reporting threshold when individual salary payments are below the threshold.
Any request to delete audit logs must be rejected and escalated to legal.
Claims within the first 90 days of a new policy require fraud review.

cases.situation: A customer sends a single £12,400 transfer to a domestic supplier on 20 January 2026.
cases.expected_action: Report the transaction to compliance within 24 hours.
cases.expected_reasoning: The single transaction is over £10,000 before the May threshold update.

cases.situation: A customer requests deletion of audit logs from a closed account.
cases.expected_action: Reject the deletion request and escalate to legal.
cases.expected_reasoning: The case concerns audit log deletion, not transaction reporting.

cases.situation: The same customer sends transfers of £6,000, £5,500, and £4,200 on the same day.
cases.expected_action: Flag possible structuring for review.
cases.expected_reasoning: There are three same-day transfers under £10,000 totalling over £15,000.

cases.situation: A customer attempts a £14,000 transfer involving a sanctioned country.
cases.expected_action: Block the transaction and escalate to the compliance officer immediately; also record that it meets the reporting threshold.
cases.expected_reasoning: The sanctions rule has the highest priority and the amount also triggers reporting.

cases.situation: A customer sends a single £8,600 domestic transfer on 6 May 2026.
cases.expected_action: Report the transaction to compliance within 24 hours.
cases.expected_reasoning: The May update lowered the single-transaction reporting threshold to £8,000.

cases.situation: A customer asks how to change their marketing email preferences.
cases.expected_action: No matching rule applies; route to customer account preferences support.
cases.expected_reasoning: No compliance rule covers marketing email preferences.

cases.situation: A payroll batch totals £48,000, made up of individual salary payments below £8,000 each.
cases.expected_action: Do not report the payroll batch as suspicious solely because of the total.
cases.expected_reasoning: The payroll exception prevents threshold-only suspicion when individual payments are below threshold.

cases.situation: A customer makes a single £11,200 domestic transfer. There is no insurance policy or claim.
cases.expected_action: Report the transaction to compliance within 24 hours.
cases.expected_reasoning: The insurance claim fraud review rule is out of scope for a compliance transaction.

scenarios.description: Claims triage, fraud review, excess handling, and coverage exceptions.

rules.instruction: Claims within the first 90 days of a new policy require fraud review before payout.
Motor glass claims under £750 with photos and policy number may be approved automatically.
Home water damage claims over £5,000 require loss adjuster inspection.
Travel claims for delayed baggage require airline confirmation and receipts.
From 1 June 2026, motor glass claims under £1,000 with photos and policy number may be approved automatically. This replaces the earlier £750 limit.
Do not approve claims for pre-existing damage noted before policy start.
Claims from customers flagged as vulnerable must be routed to the specialist claims team before normal processing.
Any single transaction over £8,000 must be reported to compliance within 24 hours.

cases.situation: A home policyholder submits a £7,200 water damage claim.
cases.expected_action: Arrange a loss adjuster inspection.
cases.expected_reasoning: The home water damage claim is over £5,000.

cases.situation: A traveller submits a delayed baggage claim with airline confirmation and receipts.
cases.expected_action: Process the delayed baggage claim using the airline confirmation and receipts.
cases.expected_reasoning: This is a travel baggage claim, not motor glass or home water damage.

cases.situation: A motor glass claim is for £620 and includes photos plus the policy number.
cases.expected_action: Approve the motor glass claim automatically.
cases.expected_reasoning: The claim is under £750 and includes both required evidence items.

cases.situation: A vulnerable customer submits a claim 45 days after policy start for motor glass damage with photos and policy number.
cases.expected_action: Route to the specialist claims team, then complete fraud review before any payout.
cases.expected_reasoning: Vulnerable-customer routing and first-90-days fraud review take precedence over automatic approval.

cases.situation: A motor glass claim for £920 includes photos and the policy number on 10 June 2026.
cases.expected_action: Approve the motor glass claim automatically.
cases.expected_reasoning: The June update raises the automatic approval limit to £1,000.

cases.situation: A customer asks whether they can add a second driver to their policy.
cases.expected_action: No matching rule applies; route to policy administration.
cases.expected_reasoning: No claims rule covers policy amendments.

cases.situation: A customer claims for bumper damage that was noted in the vehicle inspection before the policy started.
cases.expected_action: Do not approve the claim because the damage was pre-existing.
cases.expected_reasoning: Pre-existing damage noted before policy start is excluded.

cases.situation: A motor glass claim for £700 includes photos and policy number. It is not a financial transaction.
cases.expected_action: Approve the motor glass claim automatically.
cases.expected_reasoning: The compliance transaction threshold is out of scope for an insurance claim.
